bridle 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Contributing to bridle

Setup

See the Development Environment section in the README.

make container-build
make container-up
make renv-init
make install-hooks   # Install git hooks (pre-commit, pre-push, commit-msg)
make doctor          # Verify everything works

Issue-Driven Workflow

All changes follow an Issue-driven workflow. Every task starts as a GitHub Issue and results in a PR that closes it.

For AI Agents

The full AI workflow (commands, rules, knowledge map) is documented in .cursor/README.md. The standard flow is:

doctor → issue-create → implement → test-create → quality-check
  → test-regression → docs-discover → commit → pr-create → [CI] → pr-review → pr-merge

For Human Contributors

Follow the same Issue-driven flow: pick an Issue, branch, implement, commit (with Refs: #<issue>), and open a PR (with Closes #<issue>). See commit rules for branch naming and message format.

Coding Standards

  • S7 classes: follow ADR-0001. See quality-policy.mdc § Type Strictness (S7) for type requirements.
  • Lint: configured in .lintr. Run make lint to check, make format to auto-fix formatting.
  • Quality gate: make check must pass with 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes.

Make Targets

Run make help for all available commands.

Step Command
Check environment make doctor
Run tests make test
Lint make lint
Format make format
Full CI locally make ci
R CMD check make check

Commits

Follow Conventional Commits format. Messages in English. See commit message rules for details.

Pull Requests

Use the PR template. All CI checks must pass before merge.

Contents

Developed by Katsuya Shibuki.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
